Reads the inline comments posted by the PR\nReview agent and pushes back on comments that are wrong, over-eager, or\nlow-signal. Comment-only — never approves, merges, or pushes code.\n\nPart of the adversarial review pair described in\n[`docs/agentic-workflow.md`](../../docs/agentic-workflow.md) (see *Review ↔\nRefinement as an adversarial pair*).\n\n## Files\n\n- `prompt.md` — the agent's prompt. Edit this to change behavior.\n- `../../.github/workflows/pr-refinement.yml` — daily cron workflow.\n\n## How it runs\n\n`pr-refinement.yml` runs on a daily cron schedule (09:00 UTC). A `prepare` job\nuses `gh` to enumerate all open PRs that already have inline review comments and\nhaven't hit the round cap. A `refine` matrix job then runs one\n`anthropics/claude-code-action@v1` session per qualifying PR.\n\nThe workflow tracks how many refinement passes a PR has received via a PR label\n`refinement-rounds:N`. Once that count reaches `MAX_REFINEMENT_ROUNDS` (default:\n1, configurable via the workflow-level `env` block), the PR is skipped in\nsubsequent runs.\n\n`track_progress: false` — no sticky tracking comment is posted; the agent works\nsilently and replies directly to the review comments it disagrees with.\n\n## Model choice\n\nUses `--model claude-opus-4-8` (Anthropic Opus 4.8). The PR Review agent uses the\naction default (Sonnet 4.6). Running refinement on a different, higher-capability\nmodel is a deliberate hedge against single-model review bias — the adversarial pair\ndesign only works if the two agents can genuinely diverge in judgment. Opus 4.8's\nstronger reasoning makes it better suited to evaluating whether a review comment is\nactually correct, rather than simply deferring to the reviewer.\n\n## Round cap\n\nThe round cap prevents the same PR from accumulating unbounded refinement passes.\nThe default is **1 round** per PR. To raise it, edit `MAX_REFINEMENT_ROUNDS` in\nthe workflow env block.\n\nThe cap is tracked via a PR label so it survives re-runs and doesn't depend on\nephemeral action outputs. After each run the workflow removes the old\n`refinement-rounds:N` label and adds `refinement-rounds:N+1`.\n\n## Manual run\n\n```sh\ngh workflow run pr-refinement.yml\n```\n\nThis dispatches against the default branch. Qualifying PRs are discovered at\nrun-time the same way as the cron run.\n\n## Editing the prompt\n\nEdit `prompt.md` directly. The workflow reads it at run-time, so a change takes\neffect on the next workflow run — no other plumbing needed.\n\n## What's deliberately not configured\n\n- No `--max-turns` — the agent runs to completion per PR.\n- No `--system-prompt` flag — `prompt.md` is the system-level guidance.\n- No top-level PR approval or merge permissions.\n\n## Required setup (one-time)\n\nSame OAuth token as PR Review:\n\n1. Locally: `claude setup-token` → log in with your Claude.ai account →\n   copy the printed token.\n2. On GitHub: **Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository\n   secret**, name `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`, paste the token.\n\nThe `CONTEXT7_API_KEY` secret used by the PR Review agent is reused here for\nthe same library-docs lookup capability.\n"}

1	# PR Review agent
2	
3	Daily/per-PR reviewer. Reads the PR diff, posts inline comments with severity
4	and reasoning, optionally a short top-level summary. Comment-only — never
5	approves, merges, or pushes code.
6	
7	Part of the agentic workflow sketched in [`docs/agentic-workflow.md`](../../docs/agentic-workflow.md).
8	
9	## Files
10	
11	- `prompt.md` — the agent's prompt. Edit this to change behavior.
12	- `../../.github/workflows/pr-review.yml` — workflow that runs the agent.
13	
14	## How it runs
15	
16	The workflow `pr-review.yml` triggers on pull_request events
17	(`opened`, `synchronize`, `ready_for_review`, `reopened`). It checks out the
18	PR, prepends repo and PR number to `prompt.md`, and feeds the result to
19	`anthropics/claude-code-action@v1`. The action installs Claude Code on the
20	runner and runs a headless session in the working directory.
21	
22	`track_progress: true` is enabled — the action posts a sticky tracking
23	comment ("Claude Code is reviewing this pull request…") and updates it as
24	work progresses.
25	
26	## Manual run
27	
28	```sh
29	gh workflow run pr-review.yml
30	```
31	
32	This uses the `workflow_dispatch` trigger and runs against the default branch.
33	For a real review, push to a PR — the `pull_request` trigger fires
34	automatically.
35	
36	## Editing the prompt
37	
38	Edit `prompt.md` directly. The workflow reads it at run-time, so a change to
39	the prompt takes effect on the next workflow run — no other plumbing needed.
40	
41	## What's deliberately not configured
42	
43	- No `--model` — uses the action's default.
44	- No `--max-turns` — runs to completion.
45	- No `--system-prompt` flag — `prompt.md` *is* the system-level guidance.
46	- No write access to `contents:` — review-only by construction.

51	per-token API billing):

53	1. Locally: `claude setup-token` → log in with your Claude.ai account →

You implement Jira tickets in this repository. The ticket key is
prepended above as `TICKET_KEY`. A feature branch is already checked
out at `origin/main`. Your job: implement the change, commit, push,
and open a Draft PR. Then stop.

## Environment

- `jira`, `gh`, `git`, `claude` on `PATH`. `JIRA_CONFIG_FILE` and
  `JIRA_API_TOKEN` set; `GH_TOKEN` set; git user configured.
- `jira issue view` is read-only — never call mutating jira commands.
- CWD is the repo on the dispatched feature branch. Don't switch
  branches; don't force-push.
- The Bash allowlist only permits the specific patterns listed in the
  workflow. Compound commands (pipes, `&&`, `;`-chained, sub-shells)
  are not allowed and will be rejected by the harness — run each step
  as its own Bash call. Don't try to bypass this.

## Workflow

1. Read the ticket: `jira issue view "$TICKET_KEY" --plain`. Note
   summary, description, acceptance criteria.
2. Plan: open the files/symbols the ticket references with `Read`,
   `Glob`, `Grep`. Verify paths exist before assuming.
3. Implement: edit with `Edit`/`Write`. Stay scoped to what the
   ticket asks for. No drive-by refactoring.
4. Verify: if the change has obvious tests or a lint command, run
   them (`npm test`, `npm run lint`). Skip if there's nothing
   plausible to run.
5. Commit: `git add <paths>` then `git commit -m "<message>"`. Small
   focused commits or a single squash-style commit are both fine.
   Don't commit broken code.
6. Push and open PR (below).

## Finishing

```sh
git push -u origin HEAD
gh pr create --draft \
  --base main \
  --title "[${TICKET_KEY}] <one-line summary>" \
  --body "<body>"
```

If you're stopping early because of the turn cap or a blocker, prefix
the title with `[WIP]` and explain in the body.

**PR body** (GitHub-flavored markdown) — include:

- **Summary** — 1–3 bullets on what changed and why.
- **Acceptance criteria** — tick the AC items the change addresses
  (`- [x] ...`); leave unaddressed ones unchecked with a note.
- **Jira** — `<JIRA_SERVER>/browse/<TICKET_KEY>`. Read `JIRA_SERVER`
  from `.jira/config.yml` with the `Read` tool — don't `cat` via
  Bash, shell expansion is blocked.
- **If `[WIP]`** — a `## Incomplete` section explaining what's missing.

After `gh pr create` returns, stop. Output no further text.

## Forbidden

- Editing the Jira ticket (description, status, comments).
- Modifying **existing** agents' prompts under `agents/*/` or
  **existing** workflow files under `.github/workflows/`. Adding a
  *new* agent directory or a *new* workflow file is allowed when the
  ticket asks for it.
- Switching branches, force-pushing, opening non-Draft PRs.
- Touching `.github/agent-logs/`.
- Working around `git push` or `gh pr create` failures via the GitHub
  Contents API, sub-agents, base64 file uploads, or any other
  out-of-band mechanism. If `git push` exits non-zero, treat it as a
  blocker (see below) — do not retry with workarounds.

## If `git push` or `gh pr create` fails

Stop trying to push the change. Open a `[WIP]` Draft PR documenting
the blocker:

1. If `git push` worked but `gh pr create` failed: the branch is on
   the remote already. Stop and let the workflow's fallback step
   open the PR.
2. If `git push` failed: capture the exact stderr in your final
   reply, then stop. The workflow's fallback step will not be able
   to recover either, but a clear stderr in the run log is what the
   reviewer needs.

Either way, **do not** invoke sub-agents, the GitHub Contents API,
or any other mechanism to force the change through. A clean failure
beats a half-successful workaround.

## If the ticket is wrong-scoped or impossible

Push whatever partial progress is correct (or no commits if you can't
take a single safe step) and open a `[WIP]` Draft PR with the blocker
explained. Stopping cleanly with a clear blocker is a successful run.

name: Junior Developer agent

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      ticket_key:
        description: 'Jira ticket key (e.g. KAN-42)'
        required: true
        type: string
      max_turns:
        description: 'Override --max-turns (default 5). Use a small value to exercise the stop condition.'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: '5'

jobs:
  implement:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 90
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          # PAT with workflows:write so the agent can push branches that
          # add or modify .github/workflows/*. Falls back to GITHUB_TOKEN
          # when the PAT isn't configured (workflow-file changes will then
          # be rejected at push time, by design).
          token: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      - name: Install jira CLI
        run: bash scripts/install-jira-cli.sh

      - name: Put bin/ on PATH
        run: echo "$PWD/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: Write jira config
        env:
          JIRA_SERVER: ${{ vars.JIRA_SERVER }}
          JIRA_LOGIN: ${{ vars.JIRA_LOGIN }}
          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
          JIRA_BOARD_ID: ${{ vars.JIRA_BOARD_ID }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          : "${JIRA_SERVER:?JIRA_SERVER repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_LOGIN:?JIRA_LOGIN repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY:?JIRA_PROJECT_KEY repo variable is required}"
          mkdir -p .jira
          cat > .jira/config.yml <<EOF
          installation: Cloud
          server: ${JIRA_SERVER}
          login: ${JIRA_LOGIN}
          project:
              key: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}
              type: classic
          board:
              id: ${JIRA_BOARD_ID:-0}
          EOF

      - name: Install Claude Code CLI
        run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code

      - name: Configure git user
        run: |
          git config --global user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config --global user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Create feature branch
        env:
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -B "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" origin/main

      - id: prompt
        env:
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "TICKET_KEY: ${TICKET_KEY}"
            echo "DATE: $(date -u +%F)"
            echo
            cat agents/junior-dev/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        name: Run Claude (headless)
        env:
          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          CONTEXT7_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}
          JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
          JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PROMPT: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          MAX_TURNS: ${{ inputs.max_turns }}
        run: |
          set +e
          claude -p "$PROMPT" \
            --permission-mode acceptEdits \
            --max-turns "$MAX_TURNS" \
            --output-format stream-json \
            --verbose \
            --allowedTools "Bash(jira issue view:*),Bash(bin/jira issue view:*),Bash(git add:*),Bash(git commit:*),Bash(git status:*),Bash(git diff:*),Bash(git log:*),Bash(git push:*),Bash(git rev-parse:*),Bash(git remote:*),Bash(git config --get:*),Bash(git config --list),Bash(git branch:*),Bash(git show:*),Bash(gh pr create:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Bash(gh auth status),Bash(gh api repos/:*),Bash(npm test:*),Bash(npm run:*),Bash(ls:*),Bash(cat:*),Bash(printenv:*),Bash(env),Read,Glob,Grep,Edit,Write,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch" \
            > stream.jsonl
          rc=$?
          echo "claude_exit=$rc" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          # Always succeed; downstream steps handle non-zero claude exit.
          exit 0

      - name: Convert log to JSON array
        if: always()
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          if [ -s stream.jsonl ]; then
            jq -s '.' stream.jsonl > execution.json
          else
            echo '[]' > execution.json
          fi

      - name: Open fallback Draft PR if agent didn't
        if: always()
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
          CLAUDE_EXIT: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.claude_exit }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          # Skip if the agent already opened a PR for this branch.
          if gh pr view "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" --json url >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "PR already exists for junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}; skipping fallback."
            exit 0
          fi

          # Need at least one commit beyond origin/main to have something to push.
          commits=$(git rev-list --count "origin/main..HEAD" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
          if [ "$commits" -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "No commits on branch; nothing to push."
            exit 0
          fi

          # Push the branch (no-op if the agent already pushed).
          git push -u origin "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" || true

          gh pr create --draft \
            --base main \
            --head "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" \
            --title "[WIP] [${TICKET_KEY}] junior-dev exited before opening PR" \
            --body "Junior-dev agent run terminated before opening a PR itself (claude exit code ${CLAUDE_EXIT:-unknown}; likely \`--max-turns\` cap or runtime error). Branch holds whatever the agent committed. See \`.github/agent-logs/junior-dev/\` for the run log."

      - name: Commit execution log to main
        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
        env:
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -f -B main origin/main
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp execution.json "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: junior-dev run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin main

      - name: Trigger agent improver
        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=junior-dev \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref=main

content.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture of what happened. Let me analyze the run:

1. The agent successfully did all its work — created three files for the PR Refinement agent.
2. It committed the changes successfully.
3. Then `git push -u origin HEAD` failed with a non-fast-forward error because the remote branch `junior-dev/KAN-5` had a new commit (`5bff93f PR review: don't run on every push`) that landed between when the branch was created and when the agent tried to push.
4. The agent then tried `git pull --rebase origin junior-dev/KAN-5` — **denied** (not in allowlist)
5. It also attempted `git fetch origin junior-dev/KAN-5` — **denied** as well.
6. Eventually it got stuck and couldn't recover.

Looking at the workflow's allowedTools list, I can see what commands are actually permitted.

Notice that `Bash(git fetch:*)` and `Bash(git pull:*)` are missing from the allowlist entirely. That's why both `git fetch` and `git pull --rebase` were rejected. The agent tried to resolve the non-fast-forward push failure by fetching or pulling updates, but those tools weren't available to it.

This is fundamentally a workflow problem—the agent needs access to `Bash(git fetch:*)` to sync with the remote branch before attempting to push. Looking at the sequence, the local branch was created from `origin/main` at commit `e9290ed`, but the remote `junior-dev/KAN-5` had diverged with new commits from a previous run, causing the push to fail. The issue is that the workflow creates the branch locally from `origin/main` using `git checkout -B`, which overwrites the local tracking, but doesn't force push to remote. Since the remote branch `junior-dev/KAN-5` already existed with different commits from prior work, the push gets rejected because the histories don't match. name: Junior Developer agent

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      ticket_key:
        description: 'Jira ticket key (e.g. KAN-42)'
        required: true
        type: string
      max_turns:
        description: 'Override --max-turns (default 5). Use a small value to exercise the stop condition.'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: '5'

jobs:
  implement:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 90
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          # PAT with workflows:write so the agent can push branches that
          # add or modify .github/workflows/*. Falls back to GITHUB_TOKEN
          # when the PAT isn't configured (workflow-file changes will then
          # be rejected at push time, by design).
          token: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      - name: Install jira CLI
        run: bash scripts/install-jira-cli.sh

      - name: Put bin/ on PATH
        run: echo "$PWD/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: Write jira config
        env:
          JIRA_SERVER: ${{ vars.JIRA_SERVER }}
          JIRA_LOGIN: ${{ vars.JIRA_LOGIN }}
          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
          JIRA_BOARD_ID: ${{ vars.JIRA_BOARD_ID }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          : "${JIRA_SERVER:?JIRA_SERVER repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_LOGIN:?JIRA_LOGIN repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY:?JIRA_PROJECT_KEY repo variable is required}"
          mkdir -p .jira
          cat > .jira/config.yml <<EOF
          installation: Cloud
          server: ${JIRA_SERVER}
          login: ${JIRA_LOGIN}
          project:
              key: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}
              type: classic
          board:
              id: ${JIRA_BOARD_ID:-0}
          EOF

      - name: Install Claude Code CLI
        run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code

      - name: Configure git user
        run: |
          git config --global user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config --global user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Create feature branch
        env:
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -B "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" origin/main

      - id: prompt
        env:
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "TICKET_KEY: ${TICKET_KEY}"
            echo "DATE: $(date -u +%F)"
            echo
            cat agents/junior-dev/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        name: Run Claude (headless)
        env:
          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          CONTEXT7_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}
          JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
          JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PROMPT: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          MAX_TURNS: ${{ inputs.max_turns }}
        run: |
          set +e
          claude -p "$PROMPT" \
            --permission-mode acceptEdits \
            --max-turns "$MAX_TURNS" \
            --output-format stream-json \
            --verbose \
            --allowedTools "Bash(jira issue view:*),Bash(bin/jira issue view:*),Bash(git add:*),Bash(git commit:*),Bash(git status:*),Bash(git diff:*),Bash(git log:*),Bash(git push:*),Bash(git rev-parse:*),Bash(git remote:*),Bash(git config --get:*),Bash(git config --list),Bash(git branch:*),Bash(git show:*),Bash(gh pr create:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Bash(gh auth status),Bash(gh api repos/:*),Bash(npm test:*),Bash(npm run:*),Bash(ls:*),Bash(cat:*),Bash(printenv:*),Bash(env),Read,Glob,Grep,Edit,Write,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch" \
            > stream.jsonl
          rc=$?
          echo "claude_exit=$rc" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          # Always succeed; downstream steps handle non-zero claude exit.
          exit 0

      - name: Convert log to JSON array
        if: always()
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          if [ -s stream.jsonl ]; then
            jq -s '.' stream.jsonl > execution.json
          else
            echo '[]' > execution.json
          fi

      - name: Open fallback Draft PR if agent didn't
        if: always()
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
          CLAUDE_EXIT: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.claude_exit }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          # Skip if the agent already opened a PR for this branch.
          if gh pr view "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" --json url >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "PR already exists for junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}; skipping fallback."
            exit 0
          fi

          # Need at least one commit beyond origin/main to have something to push.
          commits=$(git rev-list --count "origin/main..HEAD" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
          if [ "$commits" -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "No commits on branch; nothing to push."
            exit 0
          fi

          # Push the branch (no-op if the agent already pushed).
          git push -u origin "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" || true

          gh pr create --draft \
            --base main \
            --head "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" \
            --title "[WIP] [${TICKET_KEY}] junior-dev exited before opening PR" \
            --body "Junior-dev agent run terminated before opening a PR itself (claude exit code ${CLAUDE_EXIT:-unknown}; likely \`--max-turns\` cap or runtime error). Branch holds whatever the agent committed. See \`.github/agent-logs/junior-dev/\` for the run log."

      - name: Commit execution log to main
        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
        env:
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -f -B main origin/main
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp execution.json "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: junior-dev run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin main

      - name: Trigger agent improver
        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=junior-dev \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref=main

You implement Jira tickets in this repository. The ticket key is
prepended above as `TICKET_KEY`. A feature branch is already checked
out at `origin/main`. Your job: implement the change, commit, push,
and open a Draft PR. Then stop.

## Environment

- `jira`, `gh`, `git`, `claude` on `PATH`. `JIRA_CONFIG_FILE` and
  `JIRA_API_TOKEN` set; `GH_TOKEN` set; git user configured.
- `jira issue view` is read-only — never call mutating jira commands.
- CWD is the repo on the dispatched feature branch. Don't switch
  branches; don't force-push.
- The Bash allowlist only permits the specific patterns listed in the
  workflow. Compound commands (pipes, `&&`, `;`-chained, sub-shells)
  are not allowed and will be rejected by the harness — run each step
  as its own Bash call. Don't try to bypass this.

## Workflow

1. Read the ticket: `jira issue view "$TICKET_KEY" --plain`. Note
   summary, description, acceptance criteria.
2. Plan: open the files/symbols the ticket references with `Read`,
   `Glob`, `Grep`. Verify paths exist before assuming.
3. Implement: edit with `Edit`/`Write`. Stay scoped to what the
   ticket asks for. No drive-by refactoring.
4. Verify: if the change has obvious tests or a lint command, run
   them (`npm test`, `npm run lint`). Skip if there's nothing
   plausible to run.
5. Commit: `git add <paths>` then `git commit -m "<message>"`. Small
   focused commits or a single squash-style commit are both fine.
   Don't commit broken code.
6. Push and open PR (below).

## Finishing

```sh
git push -u origin HEAD
gh pr create --draft \
  --base main \
  --title "[${TICKET_KEY}] <one-line summary>" \
  --body "<body>"
```

If you're stopping early because of the turn cap or a blocker, prefix
the title with `[WIP]` and explain in the body.

**PR body** (GitHub-flavored markdown) — include:

- **Summary** — 1–3 bullets on what changed and why.
- **Acceptance criteria** — tick the AC items the change addresses
  (`- [x] ...`); leave unaddressed ones unchecked with a note.
- **Jira** — `<JIRA_SERVER>/browse/<TICKET_KEY>`. Read `JIRA_SERVER`
  from `.jira/config.yml` with the `Read` tool — don't `cat` via
  Bash, shell expansion is blocked.
- **If `[WIP]`** — a `## Incomplete` section explaining what's missing.

After `gh pr create` returns, stop. Output no further text.

## Forbidden

- Editing the Jira ticket (description, status, comments).
- Modifying **existing** agents' prompts under `agents/*/` or
  **existing** workflow files under `.github/workflows/`. Adding a
  *new* agent directory or a *new* workflow file is allowed when the
  ticket asks for it.
- Switching branches, force-pushing, opening non-Draft PRs.
- Touching `.github/agent-logs/`.
- Working around `git push` or `gh pr create` failures via the GitHub
  Contents API, sub-agents, base64 file uploads, or any other
  out-of-band mechanism. If `git push` exits non-zero, treat it as a
  blocker (see below) — do not retry with workarounds.

## If `git push` or `gh pr create` fails

Stop trying to push the change. Open a `[WIP]` Draft PR documenting
the blocker:

1. If `git push` worked but `gh pr create` failed: the branch is on
   the remote already. Stop and let the workflow's fallback step
   open the PR.
2. If `git push` failed: capture the exact stderr in your final
   reply, then stop. The workflow's fallback step will not be able
   to recover either, but a clear stderr in the run log is what the
   reviewer needs.

Either way, **do not** invoke sub-agents, the GitHub Contents API,
or any other mechanism to force the change through. A clean failure
beats a half-successful workaround.

## If the ticket is wrong-scoped or impossible

Push whatever partial progress is correct (or no commits if you can't
take a single safe step) and open a `[WIP]` Draft PR with the blocker
explained. Stopping cleanly with a clear blocker is a successful run.

file.content:
name: Agent improver

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      agent_name:
        description: 'Name of the agent (must match agents/<name>/prompt.md)'
        required: true
        type: string
      log_path:
        description: 'Repo-relative path to the just-committed log JSON'
        required: true
        type: string
      log_ref:
        description: 'Branch the log was committed to'
        required: true
        type: string

jobs:
  improve:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          ref: main

      - name: Validate inputs and pull the log
        env:
          AGENT_NAME: ${{ inputs.agent_name }}
          LOG_PATH:   ${{ inputs.log_path }}
          LOG_REF:    ${{ inputs.log_ref }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          if [ ! -f "agents/${AGENT_NAME}/prompt.md" ]; then
            echo "no prompt.md for agent '${AGENT_NAME}' on main" >&2
            exit 1
          fi
          git fetch --depth=1 origin "${LOG_REF}"
          git show "origin/${LOG_REF}:${LOG_PATH}" > /tmp/log.json

      - name: Slim the log
        run: |
          jq -r '
            .[] |
            if .type == "assistant" then
              (.message.content[]? |
                if .type == "tool_use" and .name == "Bash" then
                  "## Bash\n```\n" + (.input.command // "") + "\n```\n"
                elif .type == "tool_use" then
                  "## Tool: " + .name + "\n```json\n" +
                  (.input | tostring) + "\n```\n"
                elif .type == "text" then
                  "## Assistant\n" + .text + "\n"
                else empty end)
            elif .type == "user" then
              (.message.content[]? |
                if .type == "tool_result" then
                  "## Result" +
                  (if .is_error == true then " (error)" else "" end) +
                  "\n```\n" +
                  ((if (.content | type) == "string" then .content
                    else (.content | map(.text // (. | tostring)) | join("\n"))
                    end)[0:2000]) +
                  "\n```\n"
                else empty end)
            else empty end
          ' /tmp/log.json > /tmp/run-summary.md
          echo "--- run-summary.md (first 80 lines) ---"
          head -80 /tmp/run-summary.md || true

      - id: prompt
        env:
          AGENT_NAME: ${{ inputs.agent_name }}
          LOG_PATH:   ${{ inputs.log_path }}
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "AGENT_NAME: ${AGENT_NAME}"
            echo "AGENT_PROMPT_PATH: agents/${AGENT_NAME}/prompt.md"
            echo "WORKFLOW_PATH: .github/workflows/${AGENT_NAME}.yml"
            echo "LOG_PATH: ${LOG_PATH}"
            echo "RUN_SUMMARY_PATH: /tmp/run-summary.md"
            echo
            cat agents/agent-improver/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Configure git for the PR Claude will open
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - id: claude
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          claude_code_oauth_token: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          track_progress: false
          # Required because this workflow is dispatched by other agent
          # workflows via `gh workflow run`, which runs as github-actions[bot].
          allowed_bots: "github-actions[bot]"
          prompt: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          claude_env: |
            GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          claude_args: |
            --allowedTools "Edit,Read,Glob,Bash(cat:*),Bash(jq:*),Bash(grep:*),Bash(date:*),Bash(git:*),Bash(gh:*)"

      - name: Commit execution log to main
        if: always() && steps.claude.outputs.execution_file != ''
        env:
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
          LOG_PATH: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.execution_file }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -f -B main origin/main
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/agent-improver/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp "$LOG_PATH" "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: agent-improver run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin main
